new_classes.definition_en: Water of heading 2201 other than mineral water and aerated water (subheading 2201 10), including ice and snow (HS Explanatory Note paragraph A), water vapour, and natural water that has been filtered, sterilised, purified or softened (HS Explanatory Note paragraph D).
Water in gaseous phase (steam), classified under CN 2201 90 00 as a form of other water not elsewhere specified in heading 2201.

new_classes.label_en: Treated Natural Water (filtered, sterilised, purified or softened)
new_classes.label_de: Behandeltes natürliches Wasser (filtriert, entkeimtes, geklärtes oder entkalktes)
new_classes.definition_en: Natural water that has undergone one or more of the following treatments: filtration, sterilisation, purification or softening, classified under CN 2201 90 00 provided it does not qualify as mineral or aerated water under subheading 2201 10.
Water in solid phase (ice or snow), classified under CN 2201 90 00 as referred to in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2201, paragraph A.

Process applied to natural water comprising one or more of the following operations: filtration, sterilisation, purification or softening, resulting in treated natural water classifiable under CN 2201 90 00.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms cover the main distinguishing criteria of CN 2201 90 00: (1) water vapour (WaterVapour class), (2) natural water that is filtered, sterilised, purified or softened (TreatedNaturalWater class linked to WaterTreatmentProcess), and (3) ice and snow as referred to in HS EN paragraph A (IceOrSnow class). All three are placed as subclasses of OtherWater, which is itself a subclass of the existing Water class and is disjoint from MineralOrCarbonatedWater (the sibling subheading 2201 10). A minor gap remains: HS EN paragraph D covers additional sub-types of purified water (e.g. distilled, deionised water) which could be further differentiated but are not separately modelled here for reasons of proportionality; they remain under TreatedNaturalWater. The BFO restriction linking TreatedNaturalWater to its production process uses a BFO process relation expressed via producedBy (not a direct part-of restriction), which is a slight approximation since the TBox does not define a generic 'produced by' restriction for water. The disjointness complement restriction is placed on OtherWater with respect to MineralOrCarbonatedWater using the complement pattern.
